title: LLM Observability, Evaluation, Experimentation Platform — Dat Ngo, Arize
summary: Dat Ngo from Arize AI discusses LLM observability challenges in nondeterministic systems where execution paths vary per run. Arize Phoenix is an open-source observability tool deployable as a single container, while the enterprise product adds an AI layer called Alex that automatically scans traces, identifies issues, and creates evaluations.
writeup: This talk from Arize's Dat Ngo covers Phoenix, an open-source platform for LLM observability and evaluation that ships as a single container. It matters because tracing, anomaly detection, and evaluation generation remain heavy manual work in production LLM systems. The key takeaways: the enterprise tier adds an AI layer called Alex that automatically scans traces, detects anomalies, and generates evaluations to cut that overhead; the platform spans observability, golden datasets, and deterministic checks while keeping humans in control.
